description_long: This is a small, single-propeller airplane with a sleek, streamlined fuselage painted primarily in gray and black, accented by orange tips on the wings and tail. The aircraft features a low-wing configuration with straight wings, each bearing roundels and identification markings, suggesting military or training use. The cockpit is enclosed with a clear canopy, positioned forward on the fuselage, and appears to be designed for a single pilot. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The landing gear is of the tricycle type, with two main wheels under the wings and a nose wheel, all fixed and visible. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the overall design is compact, suitable for light aviation or training purposes.
description: A compact, single-propeller airplane with a gray and black body, orange wing and tail tips, and military-style markings. It features a low-wing, tricycle landing gear, and an enclosed forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile, propeller, cockpit canopy, and detailed markings on the fuselage and wings.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the propeller, nose wheel, and symmetrical wing layout with orange tips.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other side's markings, wing, and tail details, mirroring view 0.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail structure, horizontal stabilizers, and the alignment of the main landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles; orange tips for visibility.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, all fixed and non-retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
